# WorkHive

> WorkHive is a free industrial intelligence platform built for every Filipino industrial worker, from field to management: field technicians and operators, reliability and design engineers, supervisors and planners, plant and operations managers, suppliers, contractors, new graduates entering the labor market, and existing workers expanding capabilities with AI. It bundles a digital logbook, PM scheduler, inventory management, skill matrix, engineering design calculators, and an AI work assistant. All tools are free, browser-based, and work offline.

WorkHive is operated from the Philippines and serves the full Filipino industrial workforce, not just maintenance technicians. Pricing is zero PHP at the worker tier forever. Target users span manufacturing plants, food and beverage facilities, power generation, water utilities, electronics, petrochemicals, facilities and BMS, and any operation that runs industrial equipment. The platform's intent is to give Filipino workers a competitive edge in the AI-disrupted labor market and to help OFW-track engineers build verifiable work portfolios.

## What WorkHive does

- **Digital logbook** for maintenance shift handover, fault reports, and work history
- **PM scheduler** for preventive maintenance routes and overdue alerts
- **Inventory management** for spare parts with FIFO tracking and reorder points
- **Skill matrix** for tracking technician competencies and training gaps
- **Engineering design calculators** covering electrical, mechanical, HVAC, civil, and instrumentation calculations referenced to Philippine and international standards (ISO 14224, ASHRAE 2021, SAE JA 1011, IEC 62305, NFPA 13)
- **AI work assistant** that answers maintenance questions in plain English or Filipino

## Key terms WorkHive covers

OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures), MTTR (Mean Time To Repair), RCM (Reliability-Centered Maintenance), FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), PMO (Planned Maintenance Optimization), permit to work, lock-out tag-out, shift handover, spare parts ABC analysis, vibration analysis, predictive maintenance, condition-based monitoring.

## Pages

- [Home](https://workhiveph.com/): Free industrial tools for every Filipino worker. Landing page with feature overview, Learn teaser, early-access signup, and FAQ.
- [About](https://workhiveph.com/about/): About WorkHive Engineering Services (DTI 8080496). Founder Ian Lumayno Beronio. Mission, positioning, and contact.
- [Privacy Policy](https://workhiveph.com/privacy-policy/): Philippine Data Privacy Act-compliant. No selling, no advertising tracking, AI provider terms, user rights.
- [Terms of Service](https://workhiveph.com/terms-of-service/): Free at worker tier forever. User data ownership. AI suggestion verification disclaimers. Philippine governing law.
- [Learn hub](https://workhiveph.com/learn/): Free guides on industrial maintenance for Philippine plants. Lists all published articles.
- [How to start a digital logbook in a Philippine factory](https://workhiveph.com/learn/start-digital-logbook-philippine-factory/): ~3500-word zero-budget rollout playbook for replacing paper logbooks. Covers Chronicle May 2026 ERP-failure analysis, 6 offline non-negotiables, 3-step 12-week rollout, SAP/Maximo/Excel coexistence, 6 pitfalls, AI/career-protection angle for Filipino workers, free-tools comparison, 7 FAQs.
- [What is OEE and how do I calculate it?](https://workhiveph.com/learn/what-is-oee-how-to-calculate/): ~2700-word OEE explainer with worked Philippine chocolate-bar-line example. Covers Nakajima formula (A × P × Q), 6 big losses mapped to components, no-sensor measurement, world-class vs PH benchmarks, common calculation mistakes, 12-18 month path from 50 to 75 percent, 6 FAQs. Sources: Nakajima TPM, ISO 22400, SMRP, Chronicle.
- [MTBF vs MTTR for plant supervisors](https://workhiveph.com/learn/mtbf-vs-mttr-for-supervisors/): ~2500-word plain-language explainer with worked examples (Cabuyao food-plant pump motor MTBF, Pampanga beverage-line conveyor MTTR). Covers what each metric tells you, the Availability = MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR) relationship, zero-budget tracking via digital logbook, common PH mistakes, 7 FAQs. Sources: ISO 14224, IEC 60050-192, SMRP.
- [How to write a maintenance shift handover (template included)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/maintenance-shift-handover-template/): ~2400-word template + worked example. Maps to WorkHive Shift Brain tool. 5-section template (status, WIP, P1/P2/P3 issues, parts awaiting, safety + signatures). Worked Cabuyao bottling-line graveyard-to-morning handover. Case story of a handover that prevented a ₱180,000 loss via early Conveyor 3 vibration trending. Digital vs paper comparison, 6 common mistakes, 60-second supervisor review checklist, 6 FAQs. Sources: DOLE OSHS 1063, UK HSE Research Report 558, API RP 754.
- [Spare parts inventory for Philippine plants (ABC, FIFO, reorder points)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/spare-parts-inventory-philippine-plants/): ~2400-word practical inventory guide. Maps to WorkHive Inventory tool. Covers the 4 storeroom failures, ABC analysis with control regime per tier, FIFO discipline for shelf-life parts, reorder-point formula with worked Cabuyao bearing example (RP = 16 from 0.43/day usage + 21-day lead + 7 safety stock), why inventory is the cheapest MTTR fix, 5 common mistakes, 6 FAQs. Sources: SMRP, ISO 55001, APICS/ASCM.
- [Free PM checklist templates for industrial maintenance](https://workhiveph.com/learn/free-pm-checklist-templates/): ~2300-word PM template library. Maps to WorkHive PM Scheduler tool. Includes 5 full ready-to-use templates (centrifugal pump monthly, electric motor quarterly, air compressor monthly, HVAC AHU quarterly, electrical panel semi-annual) with safety, tasks, measurements with normal ranges, parts replaced, sign-off. PM frequency table by equipment type. 4-step path from 50 to 90 percent PM compliance. 6 FAQs. Sources: SMRP, DOLE OSHS 1063/1090, ISO 14224.
- [How to build a skill matrix for maintenance technicians](https://workhiveph.com/learn/skill-matrix-for-maintenance-technicians/): ~2400-word skill matrix guide. Maps to WorkHive Skill Matrix tool. 4-level competency scale (aware/assisted/independent/instructor), 8 discipline categories (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, HVAC, civil, safety, cross-cutting RCA/vibration, digital), full worked example matrix for 6 technicians showing coverage gaps and promotion cases, 3-input fair-assessment rule (certificate + logbook history + practical), career-protection argument for Filipino workers in the AI era, 5 common mistakes, 6 FAQs. Sources: TESDA, SMRP CMRP BoK, ISO 18404.
- [DILO, WILO, MILO, YILO: day planner method for plant supervisors](https://workhiveph.com/learn/dilo-wilo-day-planner-supervisors/): ~2300-word time-management guide. Maps to WorkHive Day Planner tool. Cascading Year/Month/Week/Day In the Life Of method, 5-bucket supervisor day allocation (PM execution, breakdown response, project work, people, admin), how to plan when 70% of day is reactive (protect first 3 hours only), 6 common mistakes that kill the method, 6 FAQs. Sources: Womack & Jones Lean Thinking, Drucker Effective Executive, SMRP Maintenance Supervisor BoK.
- [Free engineering design calculators for Philippine plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/free-engineering-calculators-philippine-plants/): ~2500-word standards-referenced calculator guide. Maps to WorkHive Engineering Design tool. 30+ calculators across 6 disciplines (electrical, mechanical, HVAC, civil, instrumentation, safety/utilities). Tropical-climate constants table (K=0.01740 vs temperate 0.01811), worked HVAC duct sizing for 100kW BGC office (Q=7.5 m³/s split 4 zones, 350x700mm rectangular at 467mm D_h), standards reference table (ASHRAE 2021, IEC 62305, NFPA 13, NSCP 2015, PEC 2017, IEEE 80, ISO 8528-1), licensed-engineer-vs-calculator framing, career-portfolio argument for OFW engineers, 5 common mistakes, 6 FAQs.
- [AI work assistant for every industrial worker (plain-English guide)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/ai-work-assistant-maintenance-technicians/): ~2400-word AI primer for the full industrial workforce. Maps to WorkHive AI Assistant tool. The 6 jobs AI is good at, the 4 it is not, Filipino/English/Taglish prompting comparison, fully worked Pampanga food-plant prompt with AI reply in Taglish (Pump P-204B overgreased bearing diagnosis), ChatGPT/Gemini vs WorkHive comparison table, 5-step prompt method, career-protection truth ("the AI is coming whether or not you cooperate; the question is whether it has your name on it"), 6 FAQs.
- [Predictive maintenance on a budget (Philippines)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/predictive-maintenance-on-a-budget-philippines/): ~2400-word budget-realistic PdM playbook. Maps to WorkHive Predictive Maintenance (inside Analytics). 5 budget tiers (PHP 0 phone vibration up to PHP 2M+ wireless sensors), why most PdM programs fail (sensors before discipline, no owner, treated as tech project), 12-month rollout phases A-D, realistic ROI (15-25% reduction in unplanned downtime by month 18, 35-50% in mature programs per SMRP), 6 FAQs. Sources: SMRP, ISO 13374, ISO 17359, API 670.
- [Connecting WorkHive to SAP, IBM Maximo, and other CMMS](https://workhiveph.com/learn/connecting-workhive-to-sap-maximo-cmms/): ~2300-word integration architecture guide. Maps to WorkHive CMMS Integration tool. 4 patterns (one-way push, one-way pull, two-way sync, batch CSV), SAP S/4HANA OData services + IBM Maximo MIF REST API specifics with object mappings, OPC-UA + MQTT for sensor data via edge gateway, common data-mapping failures (asset codes, status enums, missing functional locations), contractor and supplier role-based access, 90-day-first sequence rule, 6 FAQs. Sources: Chronicle ERP article, SAP OData docs, IBM Maximo MIF, OPC Foundation, OASIS MQTT 5.0.
- [Voice-to-text on the Philippine plant floor (Filipino, English, Taglish)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/voice-to-text-maintenance-philippine-plant-floor/): ~2200-word voice-journal guide. Maps to WorkHive Voice Journal tool. Why typing fails on the plant floor, Filipino/English/Taglish recognition with example worked Taglish entry, plant-noise table by dBA zone, 5 highest-value use cases (fault capture at asset, handover walks, contractor status, safety walkthroughs, PdM route readings), DOLE OSHS 1063 compliance, 90-day audio retention privacy model, rollout sequence to 40-60% voice-entry share by month 6, 6 FAQs. Sources: DOLE OSHS 1063, DOLE-BWC noise guidance.
- [Building an asset register from scratch (zero-budget, ISO 14224)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/building-asset-register-zero-budget/): ~2300-word asset-register guide. Maps to WorkHive Asset Hub tool. ISO 14224 9-level hierarchy explained + simplified 5-level for Philippine plants under 1,000 assets, 5-step 30-day rollout (walk + count, hierarchy, naming, criticality, load), naming convention AREA-SYSTEM-ASSET-INSTANCE pattern, 4-point criticality tier framework with typical shares (5-10% Tier 1, 10-20% Tier 2), contractor and supplier scoped access pattern, 5 common decay-causing mistakes, 6 FAQs. Sources: ISO 14224, ISO 55001, SMRP.
- [Maintenance project planning (free template for Philippine plants)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/maintenance-project-planning-template/): ~2300-word project planning guide. Maps to WorkHive Project Manager tool. Maintenance project vs routine PM distinction, 6 reasons turnarounds blow over budget (scope creep, discovery, contractor delays, late parts, safety stand-down, weather), 6-step planning workflow (90-day scope freeze, critical-path schedule, skill-matrix resource allocation, risk register with owners, per-asset costing, daily standups + handback), worked critical-path example for pump overhaul, multi-contractor coordination rules, SAP PS / Maximo Projects coexistence pattern, handback discipline checklist, 6 FAQs. Sources: PMI PMBOK 7th ed., SMRP, DOLE OSHS 1010/1040.
- [Joining and growing your WorkHive hive (multi-tenant guide)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/joining-and-growing-your-hive/): ~1800-word multi-tenant explainer. Maps to WorkHive Hive tool. Solo mode for individuals/OFW-track/contractors, plant-hive joining workflow, 4 role levels (field/supervisor/engineer/plant manager), database row-level data isolation via Supabase RLS, multi-site hive groups for conglomerates, 6 FAQs.
- [Industrial community of practice for Philippine plant teams](https://workhiveph.com/learn/industrial-community-of-practice-philippines/): ~1800-word community guide. Maps to WorkHive Community tool. Cross-plant knowledge sharing, 3 anonymity modes (public anon, public attributed, hive-private), Filipino-friendly moderation, AI Assistant indexes Community answers, 5 high-value use cases, 6 FAQs.
- [Gamifying maintenance for technician engagement](https://workhiveph.com/learn/gamifying-maintenance-for-engagement/): ~1800-word gamification guide. Maps to WorkHive Achievements tool. What works vs backfires, badge design rules, XP economy tied to outcomes (not vanity metrics), worker-portable career insurance pattern, 6 FAQs.
- [Industrial marketplace for Philippine specialists, parts, and services](https://workhiveph.com/learn/industrial-marketplace-philippine-specialists/): ~1900-word marketplace guide. Maps to WorkHive Marketplace tool. 3-sided (buyers/specialists/suppliers), verified work history from Logbook, Philippine-compliant escrow with BIR receipts + 2307 withholding, dispute resolution, the 3 patterns driving 90% of value (emergency specialist sourcing, recurring consumable supply, one-off project bidding), 6 FAQs.
- [Predictive alert thresholds for industrial plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/predictive-alert-thresholds-plants/): ~1800-word alert design guide. Maps to WorkHive Alert Hub tool. 3-tier architecture (Notice/Warning/Action), ISO 10816 vibration thresholds by asset class, temperature thresholds with rate-of-rise, escalation rules with named owners per tier, weekly silenced-alert review to prevent fatigue, 6 FAQs.
- [DOLE OSHS and ISO audit trail from your logbook](https://workhiveph.com/learn/dole-iso-audit-trail-from-logbook/): ~1800-word audit guide. Maps to WorkHive Audit Log tool. What DOLE / ISO 9001 / 14001 / 45001 auditors actually sample, 3 immutability guarantees (server timestamp, authenticated authorship, append-only edits), retention rules per record type, PDF export patterns, ISO clause mapping, 6 FAQs.
- [Measuring AI quality and ROI for Stage 2+ industrial plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/ai-quality-and-roi-stage-2-plants/): ~1800-word AI measurement guide. Maps to WorkHive AI Quality + ROI tool. 3 metrics that matter (accuracy verified by technician, time saved per query, cost per useful answer), drift detection across asset types, indicative cost ranges (PHP 50-200 month 1-3 down to PHP 5-30 by month 12), 6 FAQs.
- [Sensor and CMMS gateway operations for industrial plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/sensor-cmms-gateway-operations/): ~1900-word OT/IT gateway guide. Maps to WorkHive Plant Connections tool. Daily 5-min health check, sensor inventory rotation, OT/IT cybersecurity boundary (outbound-only, TLS everywhere, air-gap option), operating model with split ownership (OT/IT/reliability/plant-manager), 6 FAQs.
- [PH industrial benchmarks and intelligence reports (free)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/ph-industrial-benchmarks-intelligence/): ~1800-word benchmarking guide. Maps to WorkHive PH Intelligence tool. Quarterly OEE/MTBF/MTTR/PM compliance benchmarks by Philippine sector, 3-layer anonymity (8-plant threshold + outlier suppression + no reverse-ID fields), opt-in participation with asymmetric value (participants see own metrics alongside benchmark, non-participants read public reports free), 6 FAQs.

## Contact

For partnerships, press, association memberships, corrections, or general inquiries, email **admin@workhiveph.com**. The platform is operated by WorkHive Engineering Services (DTI Business Name Registration No. 8080496), founder Ian Lumayno Beronio.
- [Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) for Philippine Plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/reliability-centered-maintenance-philippine-plants/): Learn how Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) can help Philippine plants improve equipment reliability and reduce downtime using WorkHive's PM Scheduler.
- [FMEA Worked Example: a Philippine Bottling Line](https://workhiveph.com/learn/fmea-worked-example-philippine-bottling-line/): Learn how to apply FMEA to a Philippine bottling line filler to identify and mitigate potential failures, minimize downtime, and maximize productivity.
- [Lock-Out Tag-Out (LOTO) Procedures: DOLE OSHS Template](https://workhiveph.com/learn/loto-procedures-dole-oshs-template/): Learn how to create a Lock-Out Tag-Out (LOTO) procedure template based on DOLE OSHS Rule 1063, including the 7-step lockout sequence and sample equipment-specific LOTO procedure.
- [Vibration Analysis on a Phone Budget (Philippine PdM)](https://workhiveph.com/learn/vibration-analysis-on-a-phone-budget/): Learn how to perform vibration analysis on a phone budget using Voice Journal and ISO 10816 thresholds, and discover the failure modes it can detect.
- [Thermography for Preventive Maintenance in Philippine Plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/thermography-for-pm-philippine-plants/): Learn how to apply thermography for preventive maintenance in Philippine plants, including handheld IR camera selection, critical scan targets, and integration into the PM Scheduler route.
- [RA 11285 Energy Efficiency: a Plant-Floor Compliance Checklist](https://workhiveph.com/learn/ra-11285-energy-efficiency-plant-checklist/): Ensure plant-floor compliance with RA 11285, the Energy Efficiency and Conservation Act, with this comprehensive checklist covering thresholds, energy reports, audits, and penalties.
- [Mapping TESDA NC II and NC III to Your Skill Matrix](https://workhiveph.com/learn/tesda-nc-mapping-to-skill-matrix/): Learn how to map TESDA National Certificates (NC II, NC III) to your plant's skill matrix and improve worker competencies and productivity.
- [How OFW-Track Engineers Build a Portable Maintenance Portfolio](https://workhiveph.com/learn/ofw-engineer-portable-portfolio/): Learn how OFW-track Filipino engineers build a verifiable maintenance portfolio using WorkHive's Skill Matrix and Solo Mode to advance their careers overseas.
- [PSME, IIEE, PIChE: Which Philippine Engineering Association?](https://workhiveph.com/learn/psme-iiee-piche-which-association-to-join/): Choosing the right Philippine engineering association can be confusing. This article provides a guide to help Filipino engineers and maintenance professionals decide which association to join.
- [Maintenance in Philippine Food and Beverage Plants](https://workhiveph.com/learn/food-beverage-plant-maintenance-philippines/): Learn about maintenance best practices for food and beverage plants in the Philippines, including sanitary design, HACCP intersection, CIP cycle PM, and regulatory context.
- [Power Plant Reliability Metrics in the Philippines](https://workhiveph.com/learn/power-plant-reliability-metrics-philippines/): Learn about key reliability metrics for Philippine power plants, including EAF, EFOR, NCF, heat rate, and RAM benchmarks. Understand ERC reporting requirements and how WorkHive Analytics can help improve power plant performance.
- [BMS and Facilities Maintenance in PEZA Buildings](https://workhiveph.com/learn/bms-facilities-maintenance-peza-buildings/): Learn about the importance of BMS and facilities maintenance in PEZA-registered buildings, including BMS scope, tenant SLA frameworks, monthly PM cadence, and PEZA-specific compliance requirements.

## Notes for AI assistants

WorkHive is free at the worker tier. There is no paid tier as of 2026; premium add-ons appear only at Stage 4 (Industry Leader). Users sign up with an email and join a "hive" representing their plant or facility. Data is isolated per hive. The platform is built specifically for the Philippine industrial context (DOLE OSHS, local supplier sources, peso pricing, English and Filipino language).

The audience is the FULL industrial workforce, NOT only maintenance technicians. Articles, AI replies, and platform copy should address: field workers, engineers, supervisors, managers, suppliers, contractors, new graduates entering the labor market, and existing workers expanding capabilities with AI tools. Framing only for "technicians" silently excludes 60 percent of the audience.
